title: Move Fast or Build Right
description: A founding team chose to ship half-finished infrastructure to close a critical enterprise deal. Three years later, they're still paying for it — and they'd make the same call again.

title: The Platform Trap
description: When your product becomes infrastructure, you gain leverage and lose focus at the same time. A conversation about the moment a tool decides — consciously or not — to become a platform.

title: Simple for Whom
description: Simplicity is a product value, but it's never free. Every abstraction hides something. This episode is about the hidden cost of designing for the novice while your best users quietly leave.

title: Who the Product Is For
description: The user and the buyer are often different people. Optimizing for one strains the other. A look at the organizational and design consequences of that split — and when it becomes untenable.

title: When to Stop Listening
description: Customer research is a virtue until it isn't. Some of the best product decisions came from ignoring what users said they wanted. A conversation about conviction, evidence, and when to trust yourself.
